text:
Team GBstar Charley Hull fears her chances of claiming gold in the women’s golf event may have taken a hit due to the smoking ban in place at theParis Olympic Games.

Hull is one of few golfers who smokes while competing and went viral last month at theUSWomen’s Open when she was captured signing autographs with a cigarette hanging out of her mouth.

The British No.1 was recently diagnosed with attention deficit hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and says that smoking helps her to concentrate but she will not be able to do so in Paris this week after organisers designated all venues as smoke-free zones.

Hull finished seventh at the Rio Games in 2016 but fears the ban will mess with her usual routine, affecting her chances of making the podium at Le Golf National.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for updates from the Paris 2024 Olympics

‘I do smoke on the course. It’s a habit but I won’t do it this week. Yeah, just something I do. I don’t think you’re allowed,’ she told reporters ahead of the first round on Wednesday.

‘Yeah, I think it will [affect me]. Because it relaxes me a little bit. But it is what it is.’

But while Hull’s smoking antics saw her gain an army of new followers on social media, the Englishwoman insists she hasn’t paid much attention to her new cult following.

.@HullCharleymaking John Daly proud. ðpic.twitter.com/hmp7EaiKRr

‘I haven’t gone on Instagram in about four or five months,’ she said. ‘I don’t go on social media. I just let my agent do it.’

Hull, representing Team GB alongside close friend Georgia Hall, will hope to emulate the efforts of Tommy Fleetwood who claimed a hard-fought silver in the men’s event last week and said she looked forward to playing in front of a rapturous French crowd.

‘I didn’t see it [Fleetwood winning silver] – I was actually on the golf course playing in England,’ she said.

‘I don’t really watch much TV but my friends were showing me shots and that and I thought it was great what he did. I thought he was going to get gold at one point but I bet he’s pretty happy with silver.

‘I think it will be great with the crowds. It’s always good to interact with the crowd and people watching. I love to play in front of crowds.

‘I like being entertaining on the golf course. Yeah, I’m hoping the crowds are big and a few people from England make it over the Channel.’

title: Team GB star fears smoking ban will hurt medal chances at Paris Olympics | Metro News
description: Team GB's Charley Hull fears her chances of claiming gold in women’s golf may have taken a hit due to the smoking ban at the Paris Olympics.
